content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Review found two fix-now issues: the new terminal-iteration resume fallback only works when raw backend output is directly JSON, and the primary iterative-minimal engine tests are feature-gated so plain `cargo test` does not cover the new loop/resume behavior.

## Amendments

1. finding_summary: `iterative_minimal` resume recovery still breaks for successful terminal iterations when the parsed sidecar is missing but the backend's raw transcript is not plain JSON. In [src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs](/home/master/p/ralphs/ralph-burning/src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:6321), `recover_iterative_payload_from_raw_output` reads the `.raw` file and blindly `serde_json::from_str`s it ([6328-6351]). But successful invocations are allowed to have `parsed_payload != raw_output`: [src/contexts/agent_execution/service.rs](/home/master/p/ralphs/ralph-burning/src/contexts/agent_execution/service.rs:334) returns the already-parsed payload without looking at the raw text when `parsed_payload` is non-null, and [src/adapters/process_backend.rs](/home/master/p/ralphs/ralph-burning/src/adapters/process_backend.rs:199) extracts/parses structured output separately while persisting full `stdout_text` as the raw artifact ([288-290]). The new code also explicitly treats parsed-sidecar persistence as best-effort and keeps running anyway ([src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs](/home/master/p/ralphs/ralph-burning/src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:6260)); if a run then crashes after the terminal iteration but before stage commit, resume hard-fails instead of recovering ([6572-6586]). That leaves `iterative_minimal` runs unrecoverable in exactly the sidecar-loss scenario this change is trying to support for real process backends whose raw output is not pure JSON.
   Rationale: The fallback path only works for stub-style raw JSON outputs. With Claude/Codex/process backends, a transient sidecar write failure followed by interruption can strand the run because the raw transcript is not a faithful serialization of `parsed_payload`.
2. finding_summary: the new end-to-end `iterative_minimal` engine coverage is not part of the default `cargo test` gate promised by the bead. `Cargo.toml` defines `test-stub` as an opt-in feature ([Cargo.toml](/home/master/p/ralphs/ralph-burning/Cargo.toml:6)), and [tests/unit.rs](/home/master/p/ralphs/ralph-burning/tests/unit.rs:73) only includes `workflow_engine_test` when that feature is enabled. The new coverage for the implementer loop and resume behavior lives in that gated module (for example [tests/unit/workflow_engine_test.rs](/home/master/p/ralphs/ralph-burning/tests/unit/workflow_engine_test.rs:1729)). As a result, plain `cargo test` does not exercise the core behavior added in this bead, so the default verification command in the acceptance criteria can go green while the iterative loop/regression path is broken.
   Rationale: This is a real maintainability risk: the most important behavior added by the change is only covered when contributors remember to pass a non-default feature flag, which undermines the stated default verification gate.
